title.rendered: Python for Everybody: Exploring Data Using Python 3

metadata.pb_title: Python for Everybody: Exploring Data Using Python 3
metadata.pb_description: This book is designed to introduce students to programming and computational thinking through the lens of exploring data. Python is an easy to use and easy to learn programming language that is freely available on Macintosh, Windows, or Linux computers.

A comprehensive introduction to programming using Python 3. Covers fundamental concepts such as variables, control flow, functions, data structures, and object-oriented programming with practical exercises throughout.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: python, programming, computer science, open textbook

title.rendered: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures Using Python

metadata.pb_title: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures Using Python
metadata.pb_authors: Bradley N. Miller; David L. Ranum; Roman Yasinovskyy
metadata.pb_description: This textbook is designed as a text for a first course in data structures and algorithms, using Python. The book builds on a strong foundation of core computer science knowledge and introduces students to abstract data types and data structures.
metadata.pb_copyright_holder: Bradley N. Miller; David L. Ranum
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: python, algorithms, data structures, computer science

title.rendered: Computational and Inferential Thinking: The Foundations of Data Science

metadata.pb_title: Computational and Inferential Thinking: The Foundations of Data Science
metadata.pb_authors: Ani Adhikari; John DeNero; David Wagner
metadata.pb_description: The textbook for Data 8: Foundations of Data Science at UC Berkeley. The book combines three perspectives: inferential thinking, computational thinking, and real-world relevance. Uses Python extensively for data analysis.
metadata.pb_copyright_holder: Ani Adhikari; John DeNero; David Wagner
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: python, data science, statistics, inference, computation

title.rendered: Introduction to Python for Data Science

metadata.pb_title: Introduction to Python for Data Science
metadata.pb_description: An introduction to the Python programming language for data science applications. Topics include NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and Scikit-Learn, with a focus on practical data analysis workflows.
metadata.pb_keywords_tags: python, data science, numpy, pandas, machine learning

title.rendered: Think Python: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist, 2nd Edition

metadata.pb_title: Think Python: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist, 2nd Edition
metadata.pb_subtitle: How to Think Like a Computer Scientist
metadata.pb_description: Think Python is an introduction to Python programming for beginners. It starts with basic concepts of programming, and is carefully designed to define all terms when they are first used and to develop each new concept in a logical progression.
